title: Explain what logs, metrics, and traces each tell you
description: Explain logs, metrics, and traces in observability using plain language.
prompt: Explain the role of logs, metrics, and traces when diagnosing a slow or failing cloud app.

examples.output: Plain-language differences and when to use each.

constraints: Give one example for each telemetry type.

strong_answer_includes: Logs are event records with details
Metrics are numeric time-series for trends and alerts
Traces show a request path across services
Explains how they complement each other

sample_answer: Logs tell you what happened at a specific moment, such as an exception message or failed login. Metrics tell you how the system is behaving over time, such as CPU usage, request count, latency, or error rate. Traces show the path of one request across services, which helps find where time was spent or where the request failed. I would use metrics to notice a problem, traces to locate the slow service, and logs to inspect the detailed error.
canonical_solution_summary: Logs are detailed events, metrics are numeric trends, and traces follow requests across services.
